label: BREAKING
hook: IRAN: 'TRUMP CRANKED OIL TO $120 WITH HORMUZ BLOCKADE'
stat1: 20% of world oil passes through Hormuz daily
stat2: US 5th Fleet patrols the strait from Bahrain
stat3: Iran has threatened blockade for 40+ years
tts_text: Breaking. Iran's Parliament Speaker says President Trump pushed oil prices to one hundred twenty dollars per barrel, with the U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Twenty percent of the world's oil flows through this waterway.
caption:
Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf says Trump "cranked oil up to $120" via the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

A few asterisks worth adding:

The Strait of Hormuz carries roughly 20 percent of global oil shipments daily — about 17 million barrels. Any disruption moves crude prices in hours.

The US 5th Fleet patrols the strait from a base in Bahrain. There is no formal US blockade in place; what's being called a blockade is enhanced naval presence and inspections.

Iran has threatened to close Hormuz for over four decades — across multiple regimes and crises — and has never executed a full closure. Doing so would also cut Iran's own oil exports.

The rhetoric is loud. The shipping lanes are still open.

— The Clam Letter
